title: Apply to use microdata for research | Stats NZ
content:
## Apply to use microdata for research
Find out how to apply for access to microdata in the Data Lab.
- New research project
- Change an existing project (variation)
- Set up a remote Data Lab
- Access confidentialised unit record files (CURFS)
- Add data to the IDI/LBD
- Group project pathway

## New research project
You can only access research data in our secure virtual environment, in approved facilities (the Data Lab). You can apply to access microdata from:
- the Integrated Data Infrastructure (IDI)
- the Longitudinal Business Database (LBD)
The steps below outline the application process for organisations and researchers with access to the technical and coding expertise required to conduct research in Stats NZ Data Labs. If you don’t have these skills within your organisation or community group, the group project pathway may be another option for accessing IDI insights.

#### Step 1. Prepare to apply
Have a look at our available data and existing research projects as well as any code modules to get an idea of what is possible for your research project.
The exemplar form below provides guidance along with examples of applications to help you complete the application form. Please note that the exemplar is based on an older version of the application form but continues to serve as a helpful reference for what your application should consider.
- Microdata access application form exemplar Adobe Acrobat PDF file, 1 MB

### Microdata access application form exemplar
Adobe Acrobat PDF file, 1 MB
Talk to us before applying. Call us on 04 931 4253 or email us at access2microdata@stats.govt.nz . We can advise you on:
- the feasibility of your research
- what data is most relevant to your proposal
- experts you could talk to
- likely concerns to address in your application
- how to show evidence of your responsiveness to Treaty of Waitangi and human rights obligations (see How to apply Ngā Tikanga Paihere to microdata research projects ).
Pre-application meeting: We recommend arranging a pre-application meeting with us, at least two weeks prior to the submission deadline.
We also recommend sending us a draft application form prior to this meeting to ensure your application process goes more smoothly.
Your project must meet these criteria to be considered:
- Research is for a statistical purpose
- Research must be in the public interest
- Research will be conducted by a credible team with support from their organisation
- Suitable data is available
- Stats NZ can enforce an agreement
- Research must be released publicly
- Researchers must have the skills needed to work with the data. We recommend intermediate SQL coding skills for using the IDI or LBD. Most researchers code in SAS or SQL, others use R or Stata.
We charge the following fees to cover our costs:
- Assess and approve application: $500 + GST (fee is waived for government applications, no charge for unsuccessful applications).
- Storage: 200GB cap. Over cap $1.50 + GST per GB per month.

#### Step 2: Send your application
Email your completed form to access2microdata@stats.govt.nz .
- Application to access microdata in the Stats NZ Data Lab - new project Word document, 128 KB

### Application to access microdata in the Stats NZ Data Lab - new project
Word document, 128 KB

#### Step 3: Review process
Our subject matter, legal, and methodology teams will assess your application. Data suppliers may also review applications to use their data.
The review process may take up to six weeks, but it is likely to be less. We will keep you regularly updated with progress on your application.
We'll contact your referees during this step. We may also contact you early in the process for more information.

#### Step 4: Final approval
The Government Statistician or delegated person will assess your application and give final approval.
Setup includes signing a contract and forms, confidentiality training, and setting up various access requirements in our systems.
- Flow diagram showing the process from application to final approval Adobe Acrobat PDF file, 68 KB

### Flow diagram showing the process from application to final approval
Adobe Acrobat PDF file, 68 KB

### Releasing research results
We expect researchers to share their findings with us once they’re published, so we can link to them from our research database . We can also help promote your research. Get in touch with us at access2microdata@stats.govt.nz for more information.
We check everything you want to release to make sure you've applied the confidentiality rules. See step 1 for the costs involved in this process .
Microdata output guide describes the methods and rules researchers must use.
Data and Statistics Act 2022 requires us to protect the confidentiality of the microdata we hold.

## Change an existing Data Lab project (variation)
All variations must be requested by the main contact for the project.
To request a time extension (generally up to 12 months), the main project contact should email access2microdata@stats.govt.nz including:
- project code and title
- proposed completion date
- reason for extended access to the Data Lab.
Download the form below if you'd like to:
- add or remove researchers to/from your project*
- add a new research question
- request access to a new dataset.
* If you are adding researchers who are new to the Data Lab, we recommend completing a separate application for them. This is to enable us to process your data and other requests faster, including adding researchers who are already approved for the Data Lab.
- Application to change an existing microdata project in the Stats NZ Data Lab Word document, 65 KB

### Application to change an existing microdata project in the Stats NZ Data Lab
Word document, 65 KB

## Apply to set up a remote Data Lab
If you have a project that has been active for more than six months, you can apply to set up a remote Data Lab in your own workplace. Your location and project must meet security conditions.
Talk to us before applying. Call us on 04 931 4253 or email access2microdata@stats.govt.nz .

## Apply to access confidentialised unit record files (CURFs)
CURFs are available for analysis outside of the Data Lab environment. New Zealand–based researchers can apply for access.
Find out how to access confidentialised unit record files

## Add data to the IDI/LBD
If you are interested in applying to provide new data to be added to the IDI/LBD, you will first need to submit an expression of interest form.
How to apply to integrate new data explains this process and provides instructions.

## Group project pathway
The IDI is a valuable database that enables cross-sector research in Aotearoa, but it is complex to use. Researchers need a high level of technical and coding skills to be able to get the most value out of the data. If you don’t have these skills within your organisation or community group, the group project pathway may be an alternative option.
The group project pathway is a newly developed approach that makes data from the IDI more accessible to groups of organisations with related research interests. It enables a team of organisations to apply for a shared IDI project and pool resources, contracting a third-party researcher to conduct work in a Stats NZ Data Lab.
If you have an idea for undertaking research through the group project pathway, visit Guide to integrated data research through the group project pathway to learn more.
Page updated 3 December 2025.
